skill.name: openalex-db
skill.description: Access the OpenAlex database (240M+ scholarly works) for bibliometric analysis, literature search, and citation tracking; use when you need to query works/authors/institutions/concepts without an API key.
skill.summary: Direct access to 240M+ scholarly works, authors, and institutions via the OpenAlex API for bibliometric analysis without API keys.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After AI evaluation, all 27 static analysis findings are false positives. The 16 external command detections are markdown code syntax (inline and fenced code blocks), not shell execution. The 3 cryptographic algorithm detections are markdown bold syntax (**text**), not encryption. The 7 network findings point to legitimate HTTP requests to api.openalex.org and GitHub source URLs, which are the intended function of this research skill. The system reconnaissance finding is an audit metadata file, not executable code. This skill is a legitimate OpenAlex API client for bibliometric research with no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown code syntax detected as shell execution in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer detected backtick characters as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All 16 locations in SKILL.md are markdown inline code formatting or code fences used for Python code examples in skill documentation. No actual shell command execution exists.

low_findings.title: Markdown code syntax detected as shell execution in api_guide.md
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Backtick characters in references/api_guide.md are markdown inline code formatting for API filter parameter examples (e.g., `is_oa`, `publication_year`). These are documentation display elements, not shell commands.

low_findings.title: Markdown bold syntax detected as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer identified markdown bold syntax (**text**) as weak cryptographic algorithm. Line 3 is YAML front matter delimiter (---). Lines 21 and 60 use ** for bold formatting in markdown (e.g., **concepts**, **Client entry point:**).

low_findings.title: Audit metadata detected as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The openalex-db_audit_result_v1.json file is an evaluation report artifact containing audit metadata. The detected pattern at line 155 is descriptive text within a JSON audit output file, not executable code or reconnaissance activity.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The GitHub repository URLs at SKILL.md line 7 are source attribution references in a markdown citation format. These are for provenance tracking, not a security concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP requests to external API
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE POSITIVE but LEGITIMATE: The skill makes HTTP GET requests to api.openalex.org via the Python requests library. This is the intended function - querying the public OpenAlex scholarly database. No API key required. The requests are read-only and access a public research data API.

content.user_title: Search and analyze scholarly works from OpenAlex database
content.value_statement: Researchers need to find and analyze scholarly publications without managing complex API keys. This skill provides direct access to 240M+ academic works, authors, and institutions through the OpenAlex database with no authentication required.
actual_capabilities: Search scholarly works by keyword with filters for open access status, publication year, and citation count
Look up authors and analyze their publication output and citation impact metrics
Retrieve institution profiles and compare research output across organizations
Explore academic concepts and topics and track research trends over time
Access the OpenAlex Polite Pool for higher API rate limits by providing an email address
Query multiple entity types including works, authors, institutions, and concepts
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the OpenAlex API for all queries
Does not support write operations or data submission to the OpenAlex database
Response schema depends on the OpenAlex API and field names may change without notice
Large result sets require manual cursor-based pagination which is not automated

use_cases.title: Literature review for research papers
use_cases.description: Quickly find open-access scholarly works on a research topic, filter by publication year and citation count to identify the most influential papers.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students and researchers conducting literature reviews

use_cases.title: Bibliometric analysis of research output
use_cases.description: Track publication trends over time, compare citation impact across institutions, and analyze author productivity in specific research fields.

use_cases.title: Exploratory discovery in new research areas
use_cases.description: Discover key authors, leading institutions, and major concepts in an unfamiliar research domain to gain a quick overview of the field.

prompt_templates.title: Basic work search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent scholarly works about CRISPR gene editing in the OpenAlex database.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to find recent publications on a specific research topic

prompt_templates.title: Filtered literature search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top-cited open access papers in machine learning published between 2020 and 2024.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to narrow results by access type and time range

prompt_templates.title: Institution comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the publication output of MIT and Stanford University in the field of artificial intelligence.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to benchmark research output between institutions

prompt_templates.title: Trend analysis over time
prompt_templates.prompt: Track the yearly publication trend for quantum computing research from 2015 to 2024.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to analyze how research interest in a topic has evolved

output_examples.input: Search for open access works about climate change published after 2020
output_examples.output: I found 25 open access works about climate change published after 2020. The most cited paper has over 1,200 citations and examines global temperature rise patterns. Results are organized by citation count with title, publication year, and author information for each entry.

output_examples.input: Compare the publication output of Harvard and MIT in artificial intelligence
output_examples.output: Harvard has 3,420 publications in AI with 89,000 total citations. MIT has 4,150 publications with 112,000 total citations. The top-cited paper from MIT has 8,900 citations. Both institutions show growing publication trends since 2018.

best_practices: Provide an email address when initializing the client to access the Polite Pool for higher API rate limits
Use specific search terms combined with filters to narrow down large result sets for faster queries
Validate filter parameter names against the OpenAlex API documentation for accurate results
anti_patterns: Do not query without filters when searching broad terms like AI or medicine as this returns too many results
Do not use this skill for real-time citation tracking as OpenAlex data is updated on a periodic schedule
Do not assume all scholarly works are indexed in OpenAlex as coverage varies by discipline and region

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, the OpenAlex API does not require an API key. You can start querying immediately without any authentication.

faq.question: What is the Polite Pool?
faq.answer: The Polite Pool provides 10x higher rate limits when you provide your email address in the User-Agent header. This is recommended for regular use.

faq.question: What entity types can I search in OpenAlex?
faq.answer: You can search works, authors, institutions, concepts, sources, publishers, and funders through the OpenAlex API.

faq.question: Can I filter results by year or access type?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter by publication year, open access status, and many other properties supported by the OpenAlex API.

faq.question: How current is the data in OpenAlex?
faq.answer: OpenAlex is updated regularly but there may be a delay between publication and indexing in the database.

faq.question: What is the maximum number of results per query?
faq.answer: The default is 25 results per page. You can adjust the per_page parameter and use cursor-based pagination for larger result sets.
